components.role: Inverting amplifier — gain stage with mismatched feedback diodes for asymmetric clipping
Positive-swing clipping — silicon diode clips at ~0.6V on positive half
Negative-swing clipping — germanium diode clips at ~0.3V on negative half, creating asymmetry
Input resistor — sets gain and input impedance

dsp_model.description: Asymmetric soft clipping — different thresholds for positive and negative swings produces even-order harmonics

behavioral_description: Asymmetric clipping using mismatched diode types produces a distinctly tube-like harmonic spectrum. The different clipping thresholds (silicon at ~0.6V positive, germanium at ~0.3V negative) create an asymmetric waveform that is rich in even-order harmonics (2nd, 4th, etc.), which the ear perceives as warm and full. This mimics the behavior of single-ended tube amplifiers, which clip asymmetrically due to the tube's transfer curve. The result is a thicker, more complex overdrive that sits well in a mix and has a more organic, amp-like quality than symmetric clipping. Many boutique overdrive pedals use this technique, sometimes with two silicon diodes on one side and one on the other, or mixing LED and silicon.
